{{Quote|Once again, a Mid Rim world is sacrificed in the name of protecting the Core...or Bothawui.|Narik|<ref name="Balance Point" />}}
'''Narik''' was the [[Senator/Legends|senator]] of [[Rodia/Legends|Rodia]] during the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]].

==Biography==
"Narik" was [[Rodese/Legends|Rodese]] for "light".<ref name="GCG">''[[Galactic Campaign Guide]]''</ref> Narik was the [[Senator/Legends|senator]] of [[Rodia/Legends|Rodia]] in the [[New Republic Senate/Legends|New Republic Senate]] in [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]]. He was appointed to [[Borsk Fey'lya]]'s [[New Republic Advisory Council|Advisory Council]], and voted for defending [[Bothawui/Legends|Bothawui]] over [[Corellia/Legends|Corellia]], due to Rodia's proximity to Bothawui.<ref name="Jedi Eclipse">''[[The New Jedi Order: Agents of Chaos II: Jedi Eclipse]]''</ref> In [[26 ABY/Legends|26 ABY]], during a meeting concerning the [[Yuuzhan Vong]] [[Yuuzhan Vong War|invasion]], he was outraged at the loss of the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]] worlds of [[Kalarba/Legends|Kalarba]], [[Druckenwell/Legends|Druckenwell]] and [[Falleen (planet)/Legends|Falleen]] to the enemy, citing that the New Republic favored the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]] and [[Chief of State (New Republic)/Legends|Chief of State]] [[Borsk Fey'lya]]'s homeworld of [[Bothawui/Legends|Bothawui]]. However, Narik showed his own opinions when he blatantly told [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] that [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], which was looking at potential invasion, was not his concern.<ref name="Balance Point" />

==Behind the scenes==
Narik first appeared in ''[[The New Jedi Order: Agents of Chaos II: Jedi Eclipse]]'', where he's identified as  "Navik". ''[[A Hunter's Fate: Greedo's Tale]]'' had previously identified "[[Navik/Legends|Navik]] the Red" as the name of a warlord of Rodia. He was first identified as "Narik" in ''[[The New Jedi Order: Balance Point]]''. ''[[Galactic Campaign Guide]]'' says that "Navik" and "Narik" are both [[Rodese/Legends|Rodese]] names, with "Navik" meaning "dark" and "Narik" meaning "light". ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' has separate entries for Navik the Red and Narik, with Navik the Red's entry making no reference to him being a Senator.
